raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The state of California has allocated $1.8 million to expand the University of California, Irvine’s in-prison B.A. program.",
    "The LIFTED program enables incarcerated individuals at the Richard J. Donovan Correctional Facility to earn a bachelor’s degree in sociology from UCI.",
    "Educational attainment has been empirically demonstrated to reduce recidivism and enhance workforce development.",
    "Every $1 invested in prison education generates $5 in economic returns for society, according to a recent Rand Corp. report.",
    "At least 95 percent of people in California prisons will return to their communities, and most will lack the tools to compete in today’s job market.",
    "Educational programs represent a reallocation of funds already being spent in the penal system and achieve more successful reintegration objectives.",
    "Lower recidivism is associated with less crime and improved long-term public safety.",
    "LIFTED aims to provide a real chance for incarcerated individuals to make positive changes in their lives and society.",
    "Individuals with a bachelor’s degree are better equipped to function and contribute even while incarcerated.",
    "The new funding will close the support gap for college students impacted by the criminal legal system in California."
  ]
}
```
